figures.caption: TABLE IV: Comparison across three sequential domains. Each task corresponds to a distinct domain shift.
TABLE V: Evaluation under reversed domain sequence (D(2) → D(1)).

Fig. 6: Qualitative comparison of detection results under the domain-incremental setting. (a) Ground truth. (b) Shmelkov et al. [23]. (c) Backbone-only distillation. (d) EWC [11]. (e) OGD [38]. (f) CWD [39]. (g) CL-DETR [25]. (h) Ours.

TABLE III: Ablation study on different components of the proposed framework. “Patch” denotes patch selection, “Aug” denotes domain-aware augmentation, “Feat” and “Head” represent feature-level and head-level distillation, respectively.

TABLE II: Comparison with existing detectors and CL methods under a domain-incremental setting. “Domain 1” and “Domain 2” are the two sequential domains. “Total Metric” is the macro-average over the two domains. mAP reports mAP@50. Pre., Rec., and F1 denote precision, recall, and F1-score respectively.

Fig. 4: Spatial distributions of target centers across three consecutive subsets of 500 images each (Domain 1 to Domain 3). Each heatmap reflects the local density of targets per grid cell, clipped at a maximum count of 5.
Fig. 5: Pairwise t-SNE visualizations with density contours overlaid for each domain. Contour maps highlight structural differences in feature distributions across domains.
